name: Springfield (United States)
description: A navy-blue field charged at the centre with the seal of Springfield.
history: Springfield has been the capital of Illinois since 1839 and is most famous as the long-time home of Abraham Lincoln. The municipal flag prominently features the Capitol dome and Lincoln associations central to the city's identity.

features.symbolism: The municipal emblem — incorporating the dome of the Illinois State Capitol (Springfield being the state capital), references to Abraham Lincoln (whose home, law office, and tomb are all in Springfield), and the city's name in lettering.
